name: Kung Pao Chi (Chicken with Chiles and Nuts)
recipeInstructions:
Note: Chicken should be cut into 1-inch cubes.
More chiles may be used, to taste.
Combine chicken, 4 tablespoons soy sauce, cold water, and cornstarch with garlic salt in a bowl. Stir evenly in one direction and let marinate for 30 minutes. Remove tips and seeds from chiles, then cut into 1-inch pieces. Combine the remaining 2 tablespoons soy sauce, white wine or sherry, sugar, 1 teaspoon cornstarch, salt, and sesame oil in a small bowl. Heat oil to 400°F in a wok. Add chicken and fry for 30 seconds. Remove chicken and drain off all but 2 tablespoons oil. Heat the oil and fry chiles until black. Add gingerroot and chicken, stirring and tossing together. Add soy-wine mixture and cook, stirring, just until thickened. Remove from heat and sprinkle with nuts.
